10.2. Technical Solution/CSOW
10.2.1. The Contractor will provide a CSOW. The CSOW defines/explains how the contractor will meet the tasks required to fulfill the requirements of the Statement of Objectives (SOO). The Contractor will provide a written response outlining its technical solution to execute the requirement outlined in each paragraph and subparagraph in the SOO. "Yes," "Met," and other ambiguous responses that do not provide an actual technical solution will be deemed unacceptable. The NAFI will evaluate the CSOW as part of Technical Evaluation (Evaluation Factors - LPTA). The CSOW will be incorporated into the contract award.

PCI Compliance Technical Support & Sustainment Services
Evaluation Factors for Award Selection
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)

1. BASIS FOR CONTRACT AWARD
This is a competitive Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) Request for Quote (RFQ) in which price is considered the most important factor for Contractors deemed technically acceptable. If the lowest priced quote is rated technically acceptable, no further evaluations of submitted quotes will be completed. Contract award will be made to the Contractor deemed LPTA. Quotes not technically acceptable cannot be selected, regardless of price. The Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality (NAFI) will select the quote of the responsible Contractor in accordance with the factors indicated in evaluation factor paragraphs below.

2. NUMBER OF CONTRACTS TO BE AWARDED
The NAFI intends to award one contract for the performance of this requirement.
Quotes are intended to be evaluated, and an award made, without discussions with the Contractors, unless discussions are determined to be necessary by the Contracting Officer. Therefore, each initial quote should contain the Contractor's best terms from a price and technical standpoint. NAFI reserves the right to award all, some, or none of the deliverables requested in this Solicitation.

3. SOLICITATION REQUIREMENTS, TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Contractors are required to meet all solicitation requirements. Quote validity period will be at least 180 calendar days from the closing date of this solicitation. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the solicitation may result in the Contractor being ineligible for award.

4. EVALUATION FACTORS
As part of the Technical Evaluation, the NAFI may require an oral presentation or interview from the Contractor deemed LPTA. The outcome of the oral presentation or interview may lead to an adjustment in the Contractor's Technical Evaluation Rating. The Contractor will be provided at least three (3) days advance notice, including the presentation/interview requirements and questions.

The government will evaluate RFQ submission based on the following evaluation factors:
FACTOR 1: Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW)/Technical Solution
FACTOR 2: Past Performance
FACTOR 3: Cost/Price

4.1. FACTOR 1: Contractor's Statement of Work (CSOW) (Acceptable/Unacceptable)
A Contractor's quote is deemed technically acceptable when the contractor presents a viable CSOW that is clear and include sufficient detail for effective evaluation and for substantiating the validity of stated claims. The CSOW will include at a minimum a narrative addressing each paragraph in the Statement of Objectives (SOO) (specifically Paragraphs 3-6.12). The narrative will outline the Contractor's complete understanding of the requirement and how the contractor will fulfill all requirements.

The Contractor Statement of Work (CSOW) will be incorporated into the contract at the time of award. See the SOO, for the technical aspects.

Lee County Government, Parks & Recreation is currently soliciting proposals for Recreation Management Software with real-time internet registration, to support the processes managed by the Parks & Recreation Department, including but not limited to; the daily management of administration tasks, such as program and class reservations, facility reservations, field reservations, memberships, league scheduling, point of sale, picnic reservations, payment processing, customized reporting, exporting of program information for guide creation, ability to upload and store Adobe documents and any related professional services required to implement/train new users on the software. The Recreation Management Software must support industry open standard touch screen computers, bar code scanners, cash drawers, programmable keyboard or keypads, magnetic stripe readers, customer displays, mobile smart phones, tablets, and other peripheral hardware.

Requirements – Technical: The selected software must meet the following criteria: Content Management System – preference will be given to a mature, proven well-known CMS that supports multiple secure user accounts that enable specific view/report/add/edit permissions. Software should be reliable, secure and robust, in accordance with industry standards and practices. The software must be cloud based (software as a service) platform. Ability to integrate with other software solutions via an API or other methods that would allow the potential for CRM and Web Site interfaces. Software will contain an accurate and complete audit trail that ensures efficient processing. Software must be able to identify and itemize all fees; such as program, rental, transaction, and convenience fees. Must have the ability to provide total program costs to client. Software must be compatible with the County's financial software, Tyler Munis, and post transactions to the General Ledger. Software shall include the ability to implement different security access levels, and elevated admin privileges to appropriate staff. Launch Live Date – Targeted for July 1, 2026. Software must have a comprehensive storage procedure to store all documents and records in a manner that is easily retrievable by County. Software must have cybersecurity protections and company must provide cyberliability insurance coverage.

5.1.1 Web-Based Reporting System: The contractor shall support and maintain a web-based project tracking system for approximately 400 active SERDP and ESTCP projects. The incumbent contractor currently maintains the SERDP and ESTCP Management System (SEMS), which is used to track technical and financial status of projects, contract actions, document status, financial planning, and distribution. In addition, the contractor shall maintain information from past projects since FY95 within SEMS and analyze information as needed. The contractor shall adhere to security plans for SEMS according to industry best practices. The contractor shall work with the Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) to ensure SEMS is in compliance with cybersecurity and other information management requirements. The contractor shall report findings that require action and provide recommendations of actions to the Authorizing Official. The contractor shall provide limited modifications as required by the Director to upgrade capability.

The software development for CDM should be anchored in SAFe Agile framework and DevSecOps methodologies. The program will decompose requirements into epics, capabilities, and user stories, ensuring that development teams can deliver working software at a rapid cadence aligning with SAFe Agile methodology. Automated testing, continuous integration, and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines will be central to this framework, enabling quick iterations and reducing certification and Authorization to Operate (ATO) delays. By aligning with modern software practices, Case Development Modernization (CDM) will ensure that value is delivered early and continuously throughout the lifecycle. CDM delivery includes, but is not limited to, the following activities and responsibilities:
• Identification and definition of case development product requirements (e.g. lightweight capability definitions, user-stories) based on both technical documentation and User-Feedback obtained through Human Centered Design and Agile processes.
• Assistance in the prioritization and planning of software work items within DSCA Product Service Delivery Framework, as part of managing one or more software backlogs.
• Development of Solutions Architectures which utilize emerging and/or disruptive technologies (e.g. Artificial Intelligence (AI))
• Developing web-applications, APIs, and other software consistent with concurrently developed emergent architectures.
• Developing software documentation
• Developing and implementing an improved, modern data architecture while maintaining interim compatibility with a to-be-retired database

How does this acquisition approach map to your Agile development approach and what incentives can the government provide to create more technical and schedule efficiencies?

DSCA is implementing SAFe agile methodology and is aligned to facilitate governance and decision making within DSCA and major IA stakeholders as per the established DSCA IT governance process.

A modern, agile product development methodology is required, with a strong emphasis on Human-Centered Design (HCD). The goal is to re-engineer and simplify processes, not just perform a "lift and shift."

2.0 Background
The Space Warfighting Analysis Center (SWAC) and the Space Security and Defense Program (SSDP) provide force design recommendations to the USSF via analytical studies, leveraging multiple vendors, government civilians, and military personnel.

The analysis conducted at the SWAC, SSDP, and other partner organizations informs strategic decisions on force structure and employment and are based on MSP&A utilizing models of varied fidelity. For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

1.5. Place of Performance

Normal duty tasks identified within this work statement shall be accomplished at the Naval Research Laboratory, BPTF located at 10050 Blossom Point Road, Welcome, Maryland, 20693. Occasional travel may be required to government facilities or contractor facilities at remote sites both inside and outside the continental United States in order to perform ground command and control tasks as well as to maintain geographically separated remote antennas and associated infrastructure that are part of the BPTF baseline.

Due to the mission-critical nature of the work, security, operations, systems administration, and engineering functions are expected to maintain a daily onsite presence to ensure uninterrupted, real-time operational and mission support, technical integration, and compliance with operational and cybersecurity requirements.

The contractor shall submit all travel requirements (including plans, agenda, itinerary and dates) for pre-approval to the contracting officer's representative and is on a strictly cost reimbursable basis.

The contractor shall bill costs for travel in accordance with FAR 31.205-46 Travel Costs (subject to local policy & procedures).

The base period of performance runs from 05 MAY 2026 to 04 NOV 2026, constituting a Proof of Concept phase lasting approximately six months before optional continuation periods begin.

6 Challenge-Based Acquisition
ClaimsCore will be procured through a multi-phase challenge process. The objective is to create an environment where acquisition decisions are based on experimentation and demonstration of existing or emerging technology through a rapid and impactful assessment of commercial solutions and competing "prototypes" (i.e., limited implementations).

CMS contemplates multiple awards including a few contracts – perhaps as many as four or perhaps only one. Due to the operational cost and burden of a PoC, fewer awards are preferred. Accordingly, CMS has established an aggressive base-period (POC Phase) affordability objective. CMS encourages bidders to propose the best Proof of Concept solution possible at the lowest possible cost. Those vendors will begin work under a proof-of-concept (POC) phase where their solutions are tested against real CMS data and measured against CMS-established success criteria and metrics.

After the POC phase, CMS anticipates the down-select of contractors by not exercising option(s) for further work based on unilateral determination of CMS' best interest. This approach ensures solutions are validated in practice, stakeholder confidence is built incrementally, and only Contractors with proven capability at scale advance.

7 Proof of Concept Scope
During the challenge, contractor solutions will be run in parallel with CMS's legacy shared systems. This head-to-head approach ensures that performance, accuracy, and reliability can be directly measured against current operations. Contractors must demonstrate that their platforms can adjudicate Part A, Part B and DME claims in near real-time, deliver outputs consistent with legacy results, and provide transparent explanations for any variances. Success will be evaluated based on clear, measurable metrics defined in the POC Metrics table and QASP including accuracy, throughput, interoperability, and stakeholder satisfaction. CMS anticipates that only one solution will advance to production.

PoC scope may include, but is not limited to:
1. Adjudicating large claim volumes quickly and accurately. Accuracy will be measured through both CMS and contractor tools that compare adjudication outcomes and artifacts with the legacy system.
2. Successfully integrating with Tier 1 Systems as described below.
3. Demonstrating user interfaces and operational workflows (including both end-user and administrative functions) relevant to the PoC claim scope including any pre-configured Original Medicare rule sets, workflows, or configuration.
4. Demonstrate the 276/277 X12 transaction flow is happening accurately and in near real-time.

2.0 Task Order 01 Scope

The Post Award Kickoff Conference will serve as the official launch of the NITE-STAR Capability Development MA IDIQ. The purpose is to achieve a clear and mutual understanding of contract requirements, establish communication channels, and set expectations for successful contract execution. Absolutely no changes to the contract will be made, nor contract terms renegotiated. The meeting will also serve as a forum for questions and clarifications.

2.1 Post Award Kickoff Conference

2.1.1 Each Contractor shall participate in a Post Award Kickoff Conference, to be held in Colorado Springs, CO no later than 30 days after contract award. The Post Award Kickoff Conference will be scheduled for approximately 90 minutes, conducted face-to-face, and will not include other contractors from the IDIQ contractor pool.

2.1.2 Each Contractor will be awarded a firm-fixed price TO valued at $10,000 to complete TO 01.

(a) By execution of a contract, the Small Business Administration (SBA) agrees to the following:
(1) To furnish the supplies or services set forth in the contract according to the specifications and the terms and conditions by subcontracting with the Offeror who has been determined an eligible concern pursuant to the provisions of section 8(a) of the Small Business Act, as amended (15 U.S.C.637(a)).
(2) Except for novation agreements, delegates to the 127 MSC the responsibility for administering the contract with complete authority to take any action on behalf of the Government under the terms and conditions of the contract; provided, however that the contracting agency shall give advance notice to the SBA before it issues a final notice terminating the right of the subcontractor to proceed with further performance, either in whole or in part, under the contract.
(3) That payments to be made under the contract will be made directly to the subcontractor by the contracting activity.
(4) To notify the 127 MSC. Contracting Officer immediately upon notification by the subcontractor that the owner or owners upon whom 8(a) eligibility was based plan to relinquish ownership or control of the concern.

Market Research: In accordance with FAR Part 10, market research was conducted from 27 March 2025 to 08 April 2025. Market research on NASA SEWP identified eight (8) Service Women Owned Small Businesses (WOSBs) that are approved AWS resellers. Adequate price competition can be achieved for this requirement on NASA SEWP through WOSB competition. Therefore, it is the determination of the Contracting Officer to compete this requirement as a 100% WOSB set aside.
